ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific code for seekh kebab roll; A0CKM (meat-based dish) is the closest defensible category anchor. Flatbread wrap component is subsumed.

serving.natural_language_portion: One mutton seekh kebab roll weighing approximately 180 g, comprising one or two seekh kebab skewers wrapped in a thin roti or paratha with chutney and sliced onion.

nutrient_source.title: Mutton Seek Kabab Roll — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Input record carries HPB provenance at confidence 0.95 with review_status reviewed. All per_100g macros and micronutrients are taken directly from the HPB Foodo entry for Mutton Seek Kabab Roll (cr_id G00010902). Saturated fat is not reported by HPB for this entry and is estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for ground lamb, cooked (NDB 17174), proportionally adjusted to the fat content reported by HPB; see notes.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for mutton seekh kebab roll as a composite dish. GI estimated at 62 (medium) based on the dominant carbohydrate vehicle: thin wheat flatbread (roti/chapati), which has a published GI of approximately 62 per University of Sydney GI Database (chapati, wheat flour). The mutton kebab filling contributes negligible carbohydrate and does not materially alter the GI. Glycemic load calculated as: GI (62) × carbohydrate per serving (7.44 g/100g × 180 g serving = 13.4 g) / 100 = 8.3 g GL per serving.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 467 mg per 100 g with low potassium-to-sodium ratio
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to half a roll and request reduced salt in the kebab mixture, as the 467 mg sodium per 100 g combined with only 210 mg potassium per 100 g produces an unfavourable sodium-to-potassium ratio that can elevate blood pressure; high-sodium chutneys should be avoided.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate carbohydrate (7.44 g/100 g) with medium GI flatbread wrap; high protein slows glucose absorption
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes can consume one roll in moderation, preferring thin roti over paratha to reduce total carbohydrate load, avoiding sweet chutneys, and accounting for the full 13–15 g carbohydrate per serving within their daily carbohydrate budget, as the high protein content (14.05 g/100 g) partially attenuates post-meal glucose rise.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from mutton (~4.8 g/100 g estimated), cholesterol 85 mg/100 g, and high sodium 467 mg/100 g
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit consumption to occasional small portions, as the combination of saturated fat from mutton, cholesterol at 85 mg per 100 g, and sodium at 467 mg per 100 g collectively elevates LDL cholesterol and blood pressure risk; choosing lean mutton preparations and thin roti over paratha reduces the saturated fat and caloric burden.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Red meat (mutton) is high in arachidonic acid; sodium 467 mg/100 g exceeds RA dietary threshold
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid or strictly limit this dish, as mutton's high arachidonic acid content amplifies TNF-alpha and IL-6 inflammatory cytokines, and sodium at 467 mg per 100 g worsens joint-related tissue swelling; substituting chicken or fish seekh kebab is recommended.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Mutton Seekh Kebab Roll is a popular South Asian-origin street food widely sold at Indian-Muslim hawker stalls and food courts across Singapore. It consists of one or two cylindrical seekh kebabs made from spiced minced mutton — seasoned with cumin, coriander, garam masala, ginger, garlic, green chilli, and onion — grilled on skewers over charcoal or a gas flame, then wrapped in a thin wheat flatbread (roti or paratha) with mint or tamarind chutney and sliced raw onion. The kebab has a firm, slightly charred exterior with a juicy, aromatic interior. Per 100 g the dish provides 192 kcal, 14.05 g protein, 11.58 g fat, and 7.44 g carbohydrate, making it a protein-dense, moderate-calorie option. Sodium is elevated at 467 mg per 100 g, which is a concern for hypertension and rheumatoid arthritis. The medium glycemic index (~62) is driven by the flatbread component. Clinically, the high protein content slows gastric emptying and moderates post-meal glucose rise, making it acceptable for Type 2 Diabetes in controlled portions, but the sodium load and saturated fat from mutton require caution for hypertension and cardiovascular disease patients. The dish is halal-certified in most Singapore hawker contexts and is a common lunch and dinner option.

confidence_reason: Core macros and micronutrients sourced directly from HPB Foodo Singapore database (cr_id G00010902, review_status reviewed, confidence 0.95). Saturated fat estimated from USDA SR Legacy ground lamb data proportionally adjusted. GI estimated from University of Sydney GI Database chapati analogue, which is the dominant carbohydrate vehicle.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (4.8 g/100 g) is an estimate derived from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for cooked ground lamb (NDB 17174), where saturated fat constitutes approximately 41% of total fat; applied proportionally to HPB-reported fat of 11.58 g/100 g. This field should be updated when HPB publishes fatty acid breakdown for this entry. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions, vitamin panel, phosphorus, zinc, selenium, and moisture data for this composite dish are not reported in the HPB Foodo database entry for Mutton Seek Kabab Roll (G00010902) and are not available in USDA FoodData Central for an equivalent composite as of 2026. 3. GI of 62 is an analogous estimate based on thin wheat chapati/roti as the primary carbohydrate vehicle per University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for mutton seekh kebab roll exists in approved sources. 4. The input record used the spelling 'Seek Kabab'; the canonical English spelling 'Seekh Kebab' is used in food_name; both spellings are preserved in aliases. 5. Halal status reflects typical Singapore Indian-Muslim hawker preparation; individual vendor certification should be verified.
